reasoning: There is currently no vaccine for Kingella kingae infection, and research is only in the early preclinical stages (identifying potential vaccine antigens). Vaccine development takes many years from this stage, making it virtually impossible for a vaccine to be developed, approved, and added to the relevant Wikipedia article by any of the 2025 dates or early 2026. The slight increase in probability for later dates accounts for the very remote possibilities of extremely accelerated developments or (more likely) Wikipedia vandalism/inaccurate edits that are not reverted before the resolution date. However, the fundamental biological and regulatory timelines for vaccine development keep the baseline probability at the minimum allowed (0.02) for the near-term.
